title: Tristania laurina
taxonomy: Tristania laurina is a species of plant in the family Myrtaceae. It is native to Australia and is commonly known as the tea-tree or peppermint bush. The genus Tristania is a part of the Myrtaceae family, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants. The Myrtaceae family is often referred to as the myrtle family. Members of the Myrtaceae family are typically found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world.

intro: Tristania laurina, also known as the tea tree, is a small, evergreen shrub native to Australia, characterized by its aromatic leaves and white flowers. The health benefits of Tristania laurina include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of this plant include essential oils, particularly terpinen-4-ol and cineole, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
